Article Title: 'France will probably beat us': Haaland cools Norway FIFA World Cup hype before Mbappe showdown
Article Text:
Erling Haaland, Kylian Mbappe

Erling Haaland has fired Norway into the knockout stage of the FIFA World Cup, but the striker is not getting carried away. After scoring twice in Norway's 3-2 win over Senegal on Tuesday, Haaland said the team should enjoy the moment rather than start talking about winning the tournament.The Manchester City forward now has four goals in the World Cup, level with France's Kylian Mbappe and one behind Lionel Messi. Norway's victory also takes them to six points in Group I, alongside France, with the two sides set to meet on Friday to decide the group winner.When asked about facing France, Haaland responded with a dose of realism."I don't care, we are through. They'll probably beat us and go on and win the whole tournament."Norway have reached the World Cup knockout rounds in their first appearance at the tournament since 1998. Haaland, who has now scored 59 goals in 52 international matches, said that achievement should be appreciated.Asked whether this could become his World Cup, he said:"Depends on what you mean with that. To qualify for the first time in 28 years and going through the group stage, yes, I would say so.""To win the World Cup, absolutely not. I think let's be a little realistic here, and let's be happy, every single Norwegian on the planet today."Norway's progress has sparked celebrations among supporters. Before the match, fans gathered in Times Square and performed the now-famous Viking Row celebration, which has become a symbol of Norway's World Cup campaign. After the win, the players joined in the celebration on the pitch.Speaking about the moment, Haaland said, "It was crazy.""Martin (Odegaard) and I talked a bit about it before the match, about whether we should join in if everything went the ? way it did, and it did, so a pretty special moment I would say for the whole of Norway."The 25-year-old compared the feeling to one of the biggest moments of his career."I think it is right up there with the biggest nights I have had ? in my entire life. I have a bit of the same feeling ? that I had ? after the (2023) Champions League final (win). It is incredibly huge. I am incredibly proud and all of that."With Norway already through to the knockout stage, attention now turns to their final group match against France, which will decide who finishes on top of Group I.
Article Summary: Erling Haaland scored twice in Norway's 3-2 win over Senegal on Tuesday. Norway are through to the World Cup knockout stages for the first time in 28 years. The Manchester City striker said the team should enjoy the moment rather than talk about winning.

Article Title: Nitish Kumar Reddy ruled out of T20Is against Ireland; unlikely for England series too
Article Text:
Nitish Kumar Reddy

India’s updated squad for Ireland T20Is:

Poll Who should get the bigger opportunity after the injury setback in India's squad? A young player should step up An experienced player should take charge

Schedule of Ireland’s T20I series against India:

NEW DELHI: India all-rounder Nitish Kumar Reddy has been ruled out of the two-match T20I series against Ireland starting June 26 in Belfast. It is learnt that Reddy's quadriceps injury, which kept him out of the 2nd ODI against Afghanistan in Lucknow, has flared up and requires attention before he can take the field again. The exact timeline for his return is unknown at this stage.Reddy was also picked for the ODIs and T20Is against England; it remains to be seen whether the injury rules him out of that series too. As things stand, he will not travel with the team to Ireland.The Indian team will leave in batches for the Ireland series. One batch, featuring Sanju Samson, Axar Patel, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Abhishek Sharma, Morne Morkel and T Dilip, left today from Chennai while some players are scheduled to leave tomorrow from Mumbai.If the selectors name a replacement for Reddy, Suryansh Shedge is the front-runner due to his impressive recent outings for India A in Sri Lanka.Reddy was also picked for the ODIs and T20Is against England; it is highly unlikely that he will feature in that series. For now, he will not travel with the team to Ireland.The 23-year-old has played 10 Tests, 6 ODIs and 4 T20Is for India so far. During the Afghanistan series, he had played two ODIs while missing the second game in Lucknow.Shreyas Iyer (Captain), Abhishek Sharma, Sanju Samson (WK), Ishan Kishan (WK), Shivam Dube, Tilak Varma (Vice-Captain), Axar Patel, Washington Sundar, Ravi Bishnoi, Harshit Rana, Arshdeep Singh, Prince Yadav, Vaibhav Sooryavanshi, Prasidh Krishna.First T20I: June 26, 2026, Belfast, 1:30pmSecond T20I: June 28, 2026, Belfast, 1:30pm*All timings in local time
Article Summary: India all-rounder Nitish Kumar Reddy ruled out of two-match T20I series against Ireland. Reddy's quadriceps injury has flared up and requires attention. The 23-year-old has played 10 Tests, 6 ODIs and 4 T20Is for India so far.

Article Title: Algeria vs Jordan, FIFA World Cup Match Result: Algeria strike twice from corners to stun Jordan 2-1 and keep knockout dreams alive
Article Text:
Algeria's Amine Gouiri, front, celebrates after scoring his side's second goal against Jordan. (AP Photo)

Jordan strike first but Algeria respond late



Gouiri completes turnaround as knockout hopes remain alive



Algeria kept their FIFA World Cup 2026 knockout hopes firmly alive with a spirited 2-1 comeback victory over Jordan in a crucial Group J clash on Monday night, overturning an early deficit with two second-half set-piece goals.The win, secured at the San Francisco Bay Area Stadium, marked Algeria’s first World Cup victory since 2014 and also ended Jordan’s debut campaign after two straight defeats.Both sides entered the contest under pressure after opening-match losses, but it was Jordan who struck first against the run of play. In the 36th minute, Mousa Al Tamari’s effort fell kindly inside the box, allowing Nizar Al Rashdan to finish confidently with the outside of his right boot and register Jordan’s first-ever World Cup goal lead.Algeria, however, grew into the game after the break and began to dominate possession and territory. Their persistence finally paid off in the 69th minute when substitute Nadhir Benbouali rose highest to meet Riyad Mahrez’s corner, powering a header into the net to level the score.The equaliser shifted momentum firmly in Algeria’s favour as wave after wave of attacks followed, with Jordan struggling to contain the pressure.Algeria completed their comeback in the 82nd minute, once again capitalising on a set-piece situation. Amine Gouiri reacted quickest inside the box as the ball dropped following another corner, guiding it home from close range after a deflection to seal a crucial 2-1 lead.The goal sparked celebrations among Algeria’s supporters behind the goal, with the North African side holding firm in the closing stages to secure a vital three points.The victory carries historic significance, marking Algeria’s first World Cup win after conceding the opening goal in a match, having previously failed to win any of their nine such encounters (seven defeats and two draws).The result also keeps Algeria in contention for a Round of 32 place. After an opening 3-0 defeat to Argentina, they now sit on three points in Group J and can still finish second with a win over Austria in their final group game.For Jordan, the defeat confirmed elimination from their maiden World Cup appearance, despite a spirited effort across both matches.Argentina lead the group with six points from two wins, while Austria sit second on three points with a superior goal difference over Algeria, setting up a tense final round of fixtures in Group J.
Article Summary: Nizar Al Rashdan opened the scoring for Jordan in the 36th minute. Nadhir Benbouali levelled the score in the 69th minute with a header. Amine Gouiri completed Algeria's comeback in the 82nd minute. Algeria can still finish second in Group J with a win over Austria.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup Golden Boot race: Messi, Mbappé and Haaland ignite scoring battle
Article Text:
Erling Haaland, Lionel Messi, and Kylian Mbappé (AP Photos)

Haaland’s rise adds new dimension to Golden Boot chase



Top 10 Goal-scorers at FIFA World Cup 2026

Rank Players Goals 1 Lionel Messi (Argentina) 5 2 Kylian Mbappe (France) 4 3 Erling Haaland (Norway) 4 4 Deniz Undav (Germany) 3 5 Jonathan David (Canada) 3 6 Crysencio Summerville (Netherlands) 2 7 Mikel Oyarzabal (Spain) 2 8 Maxi Araujo (Uruguay) 2 9 Ayase Ueda (Japan) 2 10 Cody Gakpo (Netherlands) 2

The race for the FIFA World Cup Golden Boot has exploded into life as Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland all delivered standout performances on a high-scoring day for the tournament’s elite forwards.Between them, the trio have already set the competition alight, combining for 13 goals and firmly establishing themselves at the centre of the tournament’s most watched individual battle.Messi and Mbappé struck twice each for Argentina and France respectively, while Haaland responded in style hours later with a brace of his own for Norway in a dramatic 3-2 win over Senegal on Monday night.Haaland’s double came in a blistering 15-minute spell after halftime, as he made amends for earlier missed chances — including a shot off the post and a saved header just before the break.Messi, meanwhile, continued to rewrite history, becoming the FIFA World Cup ’s all-time leading goalscorer with 18 goals after Argentina’s win over Austria in Arlington, Texas. The Argentina captain now leads the Golden Boot standings with five goals in just two matches.Mbappé kept pace in the scoring charts after France’s 3-0 win over Iraq in Philadelphia, taking his tally to 16 and remaining firmly in contention alongside the tournament’s long-standing elite.While Messi and Mbappé have years of World Cup experience behind them, Haaland is only just beginning his campaign on football’s biggest stage, with Norway returning to the tournament for the first time since 1998.Despite that, the 25-year-old striker has already scored four goals and extended his remarkable scoring streak to 12 consecutive competitive matches for Norway, during which he has netted 24 times.Germany’s Deniz Undav and Canada’s Jonathan David, both on three goals, trail the leading trio in the scoring charts.
Article Summary: Lionel Messi, Kylian Mbappé and Erling Haaland are leading the Golden Boot race. The trio have scored 13 goals between them so far at the 2014 World Cup. The Argentina captain now leads the standings with five goals in just two matches.

Article Title: Hardik Pandya joins CoE’s new performance block programme for comeback
Article Text:
Hardik Pandya (IANS Photo)

NEW DELHI: In a bid to keep the BCCI ’s centrally contracted players in prime form skill wise, the Centre of Excellence (CoE) has launched a programme called the performance block. TOI understands that this programme has opened the doors for the international players to sharpen their cricketing skills at any point of the year besides being an integral part of a player's return-to-play process after injury.Sources told TOI that Hardik Pandya has opted to go for the performance block instead of pushing to get fit for the ODI series in England. Harshit Rana is the first player to have declared completely fit under this programme.“The CoE was largely a rehab centre for international cricketers. The cricketing programmes were restricted to age-group and zonal camps. Now, India players can come at any time and have very specialised sessions to work on minute things in their game. There will be a dedicated coaching staff working with the players. Also, under this programme, an injured player can only be cleared as fit to play for India if he is playing to his potential,” a BCCI source told TOI.Earlier, an injured player used to be cleared clinically after going through some match simulation sessions. Often, the CoE didn’t have a quality pool of players to play these simulation matches.Hardik is now the first big name to go through this new programme.The selection committee and the team management are concerned about Hardik’s ability to bowl 10 overs in an ODI. He was reportedly bowling 10 overs at the CoE in the first week of June before he suffered quadriceps injury on June 9. The timeline given for his recovery was three weeks, which would have been the end of June or first week of July. The selectors named the team on Sunday without considering Hardik.“Hardik is very clear in his mind that he won’t take the field for India unless at the top of his game—both physically and cricket wise. He doesn’t want to push himself and jeopardise his body with the ODI World Cup 16 months away. He contacted CoE and joined the performance block. He wants to work on specific cricket skills besides attaining prime fitness,” the source said.According to this performance block programme, if a fast bowler is undergoing rehab, his bowling will also be assessed by gradually increasing the workload. Once the bowler is able to endure the load, it will be observed if he can perform well taking that load. If the fast bowler isn’t bowling well through a session, then his return to play may be delayed.In the case of Hardik, the CoE will be working on bowling and batting according to the 50-over format. “Hardik hasn’t played a lot of 50-over cricket in the last two years. He needs to get certain aspects right before diving back into that format,” the source said.
Article Summary: The Centre of Excellence (CoE) has launched a programme called the performance block. Harshit Rana is the first player to have declared completely fit under this programme. Hardik Pandya has opted to go through the programme instead of pushing to get fit for the ODI series in England.

Article Title: 'Cricket can unite, inspire and empower at an unparalleled scale': Jay Shah backs IOC initiative on Olympic Day
Article Text:
ICC chairman Jay Shaha with IOC president Kirsty Coventry. (Pic credit: Jay Shah's X post)

Olympic Day celebrates global sporting movement



Cricket returns to Olympics at LA28



International Cricket Council (ICC) chairman Jay Shah marked International Olympic Day by extending support to the International Olympic Committee’s (IOC) global initiative aimed at promoting health, well-being, and community engagement through sport.Highlighting the unifying power of sport, Shah underlined cricket’s global reach and its ability to bring people together across borders.“On the International Olympic Day, we are fully behind the initiative by the International Olympic Committee (IOC) to harness the power of sport to promote health, well-being, and community engagement. With more than 2 billion fans around the world, Cricket has the ability to unite, inspire and empower at an unparalleled scale. Wishing everyone a happy Olympic Day and look forward to celebrating with all of you the Olympic spirit that motivates us to move, play, and thrive,” ICC Chairman Jay Shah shared on X.International Olympic Day is observed every year on June 23 to commemorate the founding of the International Olympic Committee and the revival of the modern Olympic Games. First introduced in 1948, the day promotes physical fitness, inclusivity, and the Olympic values of excellence, friendship, and respect.The celebration has grown into a worldwide movement, with National Olympic Committees, International Federations, and organising committees hosting events to encourage participation and active lifestyles across communities.IOC president Kirsty Coventry, in a video message for Olympic Day, urged people around the world to take part in physical activity, aligning with this year’s theme.Her message supports the “Let’s Move” campaign and its central idea, “You Can Do This”, which encourages young people to overcome self-doubt through sport and movement.The occasion also carries added significance for cricket, which is set to return to the Olympic stage at Los Angeles 2028 after more than a century. The sport last featured at the 1900 Paris Olympics and will now be played in the T20 format at LA28.
Article Summary: International Olympic Day is observed every year on June 23 to commemorate the founding of the International Olympic Committee. The day promotes physical fitness, inclusivity, and the Olympic values of excellence, friendship, and respect. Cricket is set to return to the Olympic stage at Los Angeles 2028 after more than a century.

Article Title: Tom Brady makes his priorities clear hours after Gisele Bündchen's alleged Father's Day snub sparks debate
Article Text:
Tom Brady makes his priorities clear hours after Gisele Bündchen's alleged Father's Day snub snub sparks debate.(Image via Getty Images)

Tom Brady’s recent post after Gisele Bündchen’s Father’s Day snub sparks chaos



Gisele Bündchen’s post sparks outrage as many support Tom Brady



Tom Brady, the seven time Super Bowl champion, has had a pretty successful career but his past marriage with ex wife, Gisele Bündchen , has continued to dominate headlines.The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bündchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are.A few hours ago, the seven time Super Bowl champion Tom Brady took to his social media to make a post about his conversations on a recent podcast he appeared in.In the clip, Tom Brady spoke about football star Lionel Messi and how well Argentina has played in the recent few years.The caption of the post reads as, “Messi is incredible, arguably the best player to ever play the game!" 🤩 Tom Brady is a big fan of Argentina... and it's not hard to see why! 🇦🇷” and it has raised eyebrows among fans.For the unversed, just a few hours ago on Father’s Day, Gisele Bündchen posted several pictures of her new husband, Joaquim Valente, along with the children she shares with Tom Brady as she wished him on the special occasion.The caption did not mention Tom Brady and read as, “Happy Father’s Day @joaquimvalente ! Thank you for leading by example, and for the values you embody, love, humility, integrity, discipline, kindness and consistency.”This has sparked outrage among fans as many feel Gisele Bündchen intentionally did not mention Tom Brady.A fan posted, “Not a Brady fan, but if your wife chose to leave you for a yoga instructor solely because you were dedicated to your profession and wanted to play one more year, you're pointing the blame on the wrong person.”Another fan wrote, “she was leaving anyway. you don’t just realize you have feelings for your jujitsu coach because your husband decided to play an extra year.”A third fan wrote, “The number of pick me men in these comments. They really can’t stand that a woman chose herself the same way he chose himself. Man had everything and wanted to be selfish. More women should be like Giselle.”Another fan noted, “That last season was because she was leaving anyways bro, Tom said it. He tried retiring to make her happy, it didn't work so he said fuck it and went back to football for a last year…”However, Gisele Bündchen has not reacted to the speculations as Tom Brady remains silent.
Article Summary: The Brazilian supermodel Gisele Bündchen recently made a controversial post for Father’s Day and it seems the NFL legend Tom Brady has made it clear what his current priorities are. A few hours ago, the seven time Super Bowl champion Tom Brady took to his social media to make a post about his conversations on a recent podcast.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup 2026: Lionel Messi sets unwanted World Cup record before stunning turnaround to become all-time top scorer
Article Text:
Argentina's Lionel Messi (10) watches the screen during the World Cup Group J soccer match between Argentina and Austria in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Monday, June 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Ashley Landis)

Argentina's Lionel Messi, center, scores the opening goal during the World Cup Group J soccer match between Argentina and Austria in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Monday, June 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Julio Cortez)

How the missed penalty unfolded

Austria goalkeeper Alexander Schlager (1) dives for the ball as Argentina's Lionel Messi's shot goes wide during the World Cup Group J soccer match between Argentina and Austria in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Monday, June 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Sam Hodde)

Messi now owns two World Cup penalty records

A World Cup penalty story spanning three tournaments

Where Cristiano Ronaldo and other greats rank

England Captain Harry Kane has taken five World Cup penalties, scoring four and missing one.

Portugal great Eusébio converted all four of his World Cup penalties without a miss.

Argentina striker Gabriel Batistuta likewise scored all four of his attempts.

Netherlands forward Rob Rensenbrink also maintained a perfect record, converting four penalties from four attempts.

A record built on longevity and opportunity

Argentina's Lionel Messi leaves the field after the World Cup Group J soccer match between Argentina and Austria in Arlington, Texas, near Dallas, Monday, June 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Tony Gutierrez)

Argentina captain's ninth-minute miss created a unique piece of FIFA World Cup history, adding to a statistical record that now sits alongside his remarkable goalscoring achievements Lionel Messi eventually found not just one but two goals that moved him clear as the highest scorer in FIFA World Cup history, but before reaching those landmarks against Austria on Monday, the Argentina captain added his name to a very different section of the record books.The 39th-minute goal that put Argentina ahead in their Group J clash ensured that Lionel Messi moved onto 17 World Cup goals, moving beyond Germany’s Miroslav Klose. He then added a second deep into stoppage time, taking his tally to 18 and extending his lead as the outright all-time top scorer in World Cup history across both men’s and women’s competitions.Messi now has five goals in just two matches at the 2026 tournament, following his opening-day hat-trick against Algeria. With this 2-0 win, Argentina have officially secured their place in the knockout rounds, while Messi has also equalled records held by France’s Just Fontaine, the legendary striker who scored 13 goals at the 1958 World Cup, and Brazil’s Jairzinho, a key figure in Brazil’s 1970 World Cup-winning side who scored in every match of that tournament. He has now scored in six consecutive World Cup matches, underlining his remarkable consistency on football’s biggest stage.Yet the most intriguing statistical development of the afternoon had arrived much earlier when the eight-time Ballon d'Or winner missed a penalty that, for a brief period, delayed his pursuit of the goalscoring record.That miss did more than postpone history. It established Messi as the outright holder of two separate World Cup penalty records, one reflecting his longevity and Argentina's repeated deep runs at the tournament, the other highlighting a surprisingly complicated relationship with penalties on football's biggest stage.The opportunity arrived in the ninth minute after a lengthy VAR review. Lautaro Martínez had broken into the penalty area before being challenged by Austria's Xaver Schlager and Stefan Posch. While Schlager appeared to make contact with the ball, officials determined that Posch's challenge on Martínez warranted a penalty after reviewing the incident.Play continued for more than a minute while Martínez remained on the turf before the referee eventually halted proceedings and consulted the pitchside monitor.The decision gave Messi the chance to score his 17th World Cup goal and move clear of Klose immediately.Instead, his left-footed effort drifted wide of the right post.At that moment, Messi remained tied with Klose on 16 World Cup goals. More significantly for the purposes of statistical history, he had registered the third missed penalty of his World Cup career.The miss against Austria was Messi's seventh penalty attempt in normal World Cup play, excluding penalty shootouts.No player in the history of the competition has taken more.It also became his third unsuccessful penalty, moving him clear of former Ghana striker Asamoah Gyan and giving him sole possession of another World Cup record.Messi therefore now stands alone as the player with both the most penalties taken and the most penalties missed in FIFA World Cup history, excluding shootouts.His overall record currently stands at four successful conversions from seven attempts.Before Monday's match, Messi had shared the unwanted record for most misses with Gyan, whose two failures came against the Czech Republic in 2006 and in one of the most dramatic moments in World Cup history against Uruguay in the 2010 quarter-finals. On that occasion, after Luis Suárez handled the ball on the goal line and was sent off, Gyan struck the resulting penalty against the crossbar in the final moments of extra time, denying Ghana a place in the semi-finals.Messi's miss against Austria moved him beyond Gyan and into sole possession of the record.What makes the record particularly striking is how it has unfolded across three separate World Cups. Messi's first World Cup penalty came against Iceland at Russia 2018. With Argentina level at 1-1, his effort was saved by goalkeeper Hannes Halldórsson.Four years later in Qatar, he converted against Saudi Arabia before suffering another setback when Poland goalkeeper Wojciech Szczęsny denied him during the group stage.The remainder of that tournament transformed his penalty narrative.Messi converted against the Netherlands in the quarter-finals, Croatia in the semi-finals and France in the final, producing three of the most important goals of Argentina's title-winning campaign.Against Austria in 2026, another miss was added to the list.His World Cup penalty record now consists of successful efforts against Saudi Arabia, the Netherlands, Croatia and France, alongside misses against Iceland, Poland and Austria.The numbers reveal an unusual contrast. Messi has become one of the most prolific scorers in the history of the competition, yet penalties have often represented one of the few areas where vulnerability has occasionally appeared.The volume of Messi's penalty attempts separates him from virtually every other player in World Cup history.Cristiano Ronaldo, often compared with Lionel Messi across a two-decade international career, has scored three of his four World Cup penalties.The Portugal captain scored against Iran in 2006, Spain in 2018 and Ghana in 2022. His only miss came later in the 2018 tournament when Iran goalkeeper Alireza Beiranvand saved his effort during a 1-1 draw.Ronaldo therefore finished his World Cup career with three goals and one miss from four penalty attempts.Several other legendary players also feature prominently in the tournament's penalty records:Messi's total of seven attempts remains unmatched, while his four successful conversions place him alongside some of the most effective penalty takers the tournament has seen.The reason Messi owns both records is not simply accuracy or inaccuracy, it is largely a reflection of longevity, opportunity and Argentina's sustained success across multiple tournaments.Monday's match against Austria was his FIFA-record 28th World Cup appearance and came during his sixth World Cup campaign. Less than a week earlier, he had scored a hat-trick against Algeria in his 200th international appearance, exactly 20 years after his World Cup debut.Few players have played enough World Cup matches to accumulate seven penalty opportunities. Even fewer have done so while remaining central to their national team's attack for nearly two decades.That longevity has placed Messi in a unique statistical category.Before halftime against Austria, his penalty miss briefly set an unwanted record. But he quickly responded, breaking the all-time men’s World Cup scoring record with his 17th goal in the 39th minute, and again, deep into stoppage time, he added an 18th to extend his lead further and seal Argentina’s 2-0 victory.The goalscoring record will inevitably dominate the headlines. The penalty records, however, provide a fascinating reminder that even the most accomplished World Cup career ever assembled has contained moments of frustration alongside moments of greatness.In Messi's case, both happened on the same afternoon.
Article Summary: Messi now owns two World Cup penalty records. His ninth-minute miss created a unique piece of FIFA World Cup history. Messi now has five goals in just two matches at the 2026 tournament, following his opening-day hat-trick against Algeria. He has now scored in six consecutive World Cup matches.

The ICC Women’s T20 World Cup serves up a triple-header today, with New Zealand facing Scotland, Sri Lanka taking on Ireland, and Australia meeting Pakistan . With the group stage nearing its conclusion, every match carries significant implications for the race to the knockouts.Today's opening fixture at the County Ground in Bristol pits New Zealand against Scotland, with both sides fighting to keep their fading ICC Women's T20 World Cup hopes alive. Having suffered two defeats in their first three matches, neither team can afford another slip-up, making this a must-win encounter in their bid to stay in contention for a knockout berth.The County Ground in Bristol is expected to offer a balanced surface, with conditions becoming increasingly favourable for batters as the match progresses. Seamers are likely to find some assistance with the new ball, while spinners could come into play during the middle overs. The pitch also provides good bounce, allowing fast bowlers to extract extra pace and carry if they hit the right lengths.With relatively short boundaries, the venue is conducive to high-scoring contests. Teams generally prefer chasing here, as batting tends to become easier under lights and later in the game.This will be the first-ever T20I meeting between the two sides, with neither team having faced the other in the format before.The weather in Bristol is expected to be hot and sunny throughout the day, with no significant threat of rain. As things stand, no major weather-related interruptions are anticipated, ensuring a full match is likely to be played.: Melie Kerr (capt), Georgia Plimmer, Izzy Gaze (wk), Brooke Halliday, Maddy Green, Izzy Sharp, Suzie Bates, Jess Kerr, Nensi Patel, Rosemary Mair, Bree Illing: Kathryn Bryce (C), Darcey Carter, Katherine Fraser, Sarah Bryce (wk), Megan McColl, Priyanaz Chatterji, Pippa Sproul, Kirstie Gordon, Chloe Abel, Hannah Rainey/Rachel Slater, Gabriella FontenlaNew Zealand and Scotland will face off at the County Ground in Bristol today, with the match starting at 3:00 PM IST and the toss scheduled for 2:30 PM IST.The second Group B fixture of the day will see Sri Lanka take on Ireland. Sri Lanka's campaign has mirrored those of New Zealand and Scotland, with two defeats from three matches, leaving their hopes of reaching the knockout stage hanging by a thread. While they remain in contention, their chances of progressing are largely dependent on favourable results elsewhere.Ireland, meanwhile, have endured a disappointing tournament. Yet to register a win, they are already out of the race for the title and will be playing for pride in their final group-stage outing. However, Gaby Lewis' side has shown it can challenge Sri Lanka, having defeated them in their most recent bilateral series, and will be eager to end their campaign on a positive note.The County Ground in Bristol is expected to offer a balanced surface, with conditions becoming increasingly favourable for batters as the match progresses. Seamers are likely to find some assistance with the new ball, while spinners could come into play during the middle overs. The pitch also provides good bounce, allowing fast bowlers to extract extra pace and carry if they hit the right lengths.With relatively short boundaries, the venue is conducive to high-scoring contests. Teams generally prefer chasing here, as batting tends to become easier under lights and later in the game.Sri Lanka holds a 4-1 head-to-head advantage over Ireland in Women's T20 Internationals, with one match resulting in a no-result or abandonmentThe weather in Bristol is expected to be hot and sunny throughout the day, with no significant threat of rain. As things stand, no major weather-related interruptions are anticipated, ensuring a full match is likely to be played.Sri Lanka Women probable XI: Vishmi Rajapaksha, C Atapattu (C), I Dulani, H Madavi, WK Dilhari, N de Silva, Kaushani Nuthyangana (wk), Kawya Kavindi, S Kumari, M Ayodhya, Nimasha MadushaniIreland Women probable XI: GH Lewis (C), Amy Hunter (wk), O Prendergast, R Stokell, L Little, L Paul, Alice Tector, AN Kelly, Lara McBride, Cara Murray, Aimee MaguireSri Lanka and Ireland will face off at the County Ground in Bristol today, with the match set to start at 7:00 PM IST and the toss scheduled for 6:30 PM IST.The day concludes with a high-stakes Group B clash featuring one of India's biggest rivals, Australia, taking on Pakistan. Australia have lived up to their reputation as a powerhouse, winning all three of their matches so far and moving within touching distance of the knockout berth. Led by Sophie Molineux, the Australians need just one more victory to seal their place in the knockout stage.Pakistan, meanwhile, are still searching for their first win of the tournament and face an uphill task against the unbeaten Australians. While the contest appears one-sided on paper, Pakistan have shown glimpses of promise and possess the ability to spring a surprise. They will be eager to finish their campaign on a positive note and test one of the tournament favourites.Headingley in Leeds is known for offering a balanced surface. Early in the match, pacers can expect some assistance from the pitch due to moisture and favourable overhead conditions. However, as the game progresses, the surface tends to settle and become more batter-friendly, allowing stroke-makers to play freely and post substantial totals.Australia have completely dominated this rivalry in women's T20 Internationals, remaining unbeaten against Pakistan. Of their 16 meetings, Australia have won 14, while the remaining two matches, played in 2022, were abandoned due to rain. Pakistan are yet to register their first victory over Australia in the format, underlining the magnitude of the challenge ahead.The weather at Headingley is expected to remain partly sunny, with only a 27% chance of precipitation. Skies are likely to stay largely clear throughout the day, and no major weather-related interruptions are anticipated, allowing for an uninterrupted contest.Sophie Molineux (C), Beth Mooney (wk), Georgia Voll, Ellyse Perry, Ash Gardner, Georgia Wareham, Nicola Carey, Annabel Sutherland, Kim Garth, Alana King, Lucy HamiltonFatima Sana (C), Gull Feroza, Muneeba Ali (wk), Ayesha Zafar, Iram Javed, Saira Jabeen, Aliya Riaz, Fatima Sana (Capt), Tuba Hasan, Tasmia Rubab, Nashra Sandhu, Sadia IqbalAustralia and Pakistan will lock horns at Headingley in Leeds today, with the match getting underway at 11:00 PM IST. The toss is scheduled for 10:30 PM IST.The ICC Women’s T20 World Cup will be broadcast live on Star Sports(TV), while live streaming will be available on JioHotstar.
Article Summary: ICC Women’s T20 World Cup serves up a triple-header today, with New Zealand facing Scotland, Sri Lanka taking on Ireland, and Australia meeting Pakistan. Every match carries significant implications for the race to the knockouts. Today's opening fixture at the County Ground in Bristol pits New Zealand against Scotland, with both sides fighting to keep their fading ICC Women's T 20 World Cup hopes alive.

Article Title: 'We’ve been playing a fearless brand of cricket': Muzumdar stands by India's attacking approach
Article Text:
Amol Muzumdar (Photo, PTI)

Amol Muzumdar

South Africa’s six-wicket win could reshape India’s Women’s T20 World Cup campaign. India must reflect on the loss, bounce back and win their remaining two Group I matches. Marizanne Kapp, grassed thrice, proved the match-winner and exposed flaws in India’s game plan.For India to go deeper in the tournament, batters Yastika Bhatia, skipper Harmanpreet Kaur and Jemimah Rodrigues will have to raise their performances. While openers Smriti Mandhana and Shafali Verma have given India a good start, their teammates with proven abilities have failed to fire.Yastika has scored 18 runs in two matches. Jemimah’s scores of 12, 19, and 1 hardly reflect her talent or her form in English conditions. Harmanpreet, with 72 runs from three innings, will have to convert her starts and up her strike rate as well.However, India’s head coach,, didn’t think the middle-order stumbling was a worrying factor. “It’s T20 cricket. There will be stages in T20 cricket when, because of the format’s high-risk nature, lots of wickets will fall. We have been playing a fearless brand of cricket, which we have emphasised.So I guess there will be some days when we will have wickets falling in clusters. But that’s the nature of the game. It’s not much of a concern.”For a safe passage into the semifinals, India will have to win their next two matches against Bangladesh and Australia. A slip-up will mean their fortunes will hinge on the results of other teams, South Africa in particular.“We got a couple of chances in between but couldn’t take those chances. We have two matches, and this is the time to stay positive,” said Harmanpreet. “We still have two more games, and if we play to our potential, we can be on the right side of those two games,” Muzumdar stated.
Article Summary: South Africa's six-wicket win could reshape India's Women's T20 World Cup campaign. India must reflect on the loss, bounce back and win their remaining two Group I matches. For a safe passage into the semifinals, India will have to win their next two matches against Bangladesh and Australia.

Article Title: 'It was madness': Geoffrey Boycott tears into Joe Root's captaincy in second Test, thanks 'heavens' for Ben Stokes' return
Article Text:
Joe Root

Former England batter Geoffrey Boycott has criticised Joe Root's leadership during England's defeat to New Zealand in the second Test at The Oval, saying the stand-in captain appeared to have learned "nothing" from his previous spell in charge. Boycott also welcomed the return of Ben Stokes for the third Test, saying England needed him back in charge.Stokes missed the second Test while an investigation was carried out into his and Gus Atkinson's alleged involvement in a nightclub altercation with a rugby player following England's win in the first Test. Root was handed the captaincy ahead of vice-captain Harry Brook. England fielded three debutants in the match and suffered a 253-run defeat, which became Root's 27th loss as a Test captain.The England and Wales Cricket Board later confirmed that Stokes would return for the third Test. The board said Stokes and Atkinson received written warnings after being found guilty of breaching contractual obligations.Writing for The Telegraph, Boycott said: "Thank heavens Ben Stokes will be back to lead England at Trent Bridge because it appeared Joe Root had learnt nothing from his last stint as England captain."He also praised Root's batting while questioning his captaincy."Now that Virat Kohli and Kane Williamson have retired from Test match cricket, Root is the undisputed best batsman in the world. What a wonderful thing to be able to say about a smashing lad who everybody likes, including me. But having praised his batting, I take no pleasure in saying he does not have a feel for captaincy as we saw at the Oval," he added.Boycott pointed to Root's handling of Jofra Archer, saying it reminded him of England's 2019 tour of New Zealand, when Archer was asked to bowl long spells and spend extended periods bowling short-pitched deliveries."Captaincy is a gift and a good leader has a natural inkling about who to bowl and what field to set for different batsmen," Boycott wrote. "You cannot read it in a book but you can learn from watching opposition captains from all over the world. Sometimes it is intuition or luck and occasionally when you get a guy who is lucky and good then you get an exceptionally successful leader. Stokes is very good at handling Jofra Archer whereas Joe seemed to make the same mistakes at the Oval as he did when he captained England in New Zealand in 2019," he added.Boycott was particularly critical of Archer's workload late on the opening day."It was madness Joe asking him to bowl an eight-over spell at the end of the first day at the Oval with numerous short balls aimed at the New Zealand batsman Glenn Phillips and the tail-enders," Boycott wrote.
Article Summary: Geoffrey Boycott criticises Joe Root's leadership during England's defeat to New Zealand in the second Test at The Oval. Boycott says Root appeared to have learned "nothing" from his previous spell in charge. He welcomes the return of Ben Stokes for the third Test at Trent Bridge.

Article Title: Will Chennai Super Kings have a new head coach next season? Big update emerges
Article Text:
Chennai Super Kings' head coach Stephen Fleming (ANI Photo)

Despite growing speculation surrounding Chennai Super Kings ' leadership structure after another underwhelming IPL campaign, the franchise is not expected to part ways with head coach Stephen Fleming . According to Cricbuzz, the former New Zealand captain will continue in the role as CSK look to rebuild following two difficult seasons.Fleming has been a central figure in the franchise's journey since the IPL began in 2008. After representing the side as a player in the inaugural season, he took over as head coach in 2009 and has since overseen one of the most successful periods in IPL history. That long-standing association appears set to continue, with the franchise maintaining its faith in him despite recent criticism.CSK's performances over the last two seasons have fallen well below their usual standards. The five-time champions finished at the bottom of the table in 2025 and followed it up with an eighth-place finish in the recently concluded campaign. Those results sparked frustration among sections of the fanbase, with some supporters even launching "Sack Fleming" campaigns on social media.However, the franchise is not planning a change at the top. Fleming's contract, which is renewed on a year-to-year basis, is expected to be extended once again, continuing a pattern that has existed for several seasons.At present, Fleming is in the United States overseeing Texas Super Kings, Chennai's sister franchise in Major League Cricket, where he also serves as head coach. CSK are expected to conduct a detailed assessment of their IPL season after the conclusion of the ongoing MLC campaign, with several important decisions likely to be discussed. Fleming's future, however, is not expected to be among the topics under serious consideration, as his continuation is viewed internally as a certainty.The development once again underlines the influence of MS Dhoni within the CSK setup. While Dhoni 's standing within the franchise has never been in doubt, the decision to continue with Fleming further highlights the strength of their long-standing professional relationship. The pair have worked closely together for well over a decade and remain key figures in shaping the team's direction.
Article Summary: Chennai Super Kings are not expected to part ways with head coach Stephen Fleming. The former New Zealand captain will continue in the role as CSK look to rebuild following two difficult seasons. Fleming has been a central figure in the franchise's journey since the IPL began in 2008.

Article Title: India vs England T20Is: James Coles gets maiden call-up in Harry Brook-led 17-memeber side - see full squad here
Article Text:
James Coles (Photo by Gareth Copley/Getty Images)

Poll Do you think England’s injured players’ absence will impact their T20I series against India? Yes, it could affect the team No, they have enough depth

England squad vs India T20Is:

NEW DELHI: England have announced a 17-member squad for the five-match T20I series against India starting on July 1, with 22-year-old all-rounder James Coles receiving his first international call-up.Harry Brook will captain the side, while Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood return after missing the T20 World Cup earlier this year. Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton are unavailable because of injuries.Coles, a left-arm spin-bowling all-rounder, has impressed in domestic cricket, the SA20 and with the England Lions. He was also the most expensive signing in the 2026 Men's Hundred and has scored over 1,300 runs in T20 cricket while taking 53 wickets.National selector Marcus North said the youngster had earned his opportunity through consistent performances over the last year.“James Coles is an exciting addition and has earned his place following his performances with the England Lions and in T20 competitions here and abroad during the past 12 months," said North.“We have selected a larger squad to accommodate for the fact that this series will begin shortly after the Test series against New Zealand is due to conclude which allows us to be flexible."England will have little time to rest before the India series, with Harry Brook and Jacob Bethell potentially playing the first T20I less than 48 hours after the conclusion of the third Test against New Zealand.The five-match series begins in Durham on July 1 before moving to Manchester, Nottingham, Bristol and Southampton. Jos Buttler, Jofra Archer, Phil Salt, Will Jacks and Adil Rashid are among the key names included in the squad.Harry Brook (Captain), Rehan Ahmed, Jofra Archer, Sonny Baker, Tom Banton, Jacob Bethell, Jos Buttler, James Coles, Jordan Cox, Sam Curran, Liam Dawson, Will Jacks, Saqib Mahmood, Adil Rashid, Phil Salt, Josh Tongue and Luke Wood
Article Summary: England have announced a 17-member squad for the five-match T20I series against India starting on July 1. James Coles receives his first international call-up. Brydon Carse and Jamie Overton are unavailable because of injuries. Jordan Cox, Sonny Baker and Saqib Mahmood return after missing the T20 World Cup earlier this year.

Article Title: Three new faces in Ireland for India series; Lorcan Tucker named new T20I captain - see full squad
Article Text:
Ireland's Lorcan Tucker (AP Photo)

Poll Will the absence of senior players negatively impact Ireland's performance? Yes, it could be a challenge No, new players can step up

Ireland have named wicketkeeper-batter Lorcan Tucker as their new T20I captain ahead of the two-match series against India, with the 29-year-old taking over from Paul Stirling Tucker, who has previously served as vice-captain and stood in as skipper twice, said leading his country was a dream come true and vowed to lead by example."It's obviously a great privilege, and not one I thought would arrive when I started my career all those years ago," Tucker said. "There's a feeling of responsibility to the playing group and organisation to represent them well, and also to all the other captains who came before me."Tucker said his focus would be on helping the next generation of Irish cricketers establish themselves, with an eye on the 2028 T20 World Cup.Despite balancing captaincy with wicketkeeping and batting responsibilities, Tucker expressed confidence in managing the demands. He also welcomed the opportunity to test his side against the reigning T20 world champions."India are an absolute powerhouse when it comes to T20 cricket. We are fortunate to be able to measure ourselves against them right at the start of our new cycle, and there will be no tougher challenge for our group than what they will offer," he added.“I hope that we can spoil a few of those parties they might have planned and show that we also are here to make an impact."Ireland will head into the series with a depleted squad after injuries ruled out several senior players, including Josh Little, Mark Adair , Paul Stirling and Curtis Campher . The absences have opened the door for newcomers Matthew Hollard, Jai Moondra and Reuben Wilson.National selector Andrew White said, “One player’s misfortune is another player's opportunity.”
Article Summary: Ireland have named wicketkeeper-batter Lorcan Tucker as their new T20I captain ahead of the two-match series against India. Tucker takes over from Paul Stirling Tucker, who has previously served as vice-captain and stood in as skipper twice. Ireland will head into the series with a depleted squad after injuries ruled out several senior players.

Article Title: Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson nightclub controversy: ECB delivers final verdict
Article Text:
England's Gus Atkinson and Ben Stokes (AP Photo)

Poll Do you think Ben Stokes and Gus Atkinson’s return will boost England’s chances in the final Test? Yes No

England captain Ben Stokes and fast bowler Gus Atkinson have been cleared of any wrongdoing following an investigation into a nightclub incident that occurred after their team's victory over New Zealand earlier this month.The pair had already been handed written warnings by the England and Wales Cricket Board (ECB) for breaching a team curfew and were omitted from the second Test against New Zealand. However, both players are expected to return for the series-deciding third Test, which begins on Thursday at Trent Bridge in Nottingham.On Monday, the independent Cricket Regulator confirmed that no disciplinary action would be taken against either player after completing its review of the matter.The controversy stemmed from events following England's win in the opening Test, when Stokes and Atkinson stayed out beyond the team's midnight curfew and visited a London nightclub. During the same evening, a team security official was reportedly struck by a rugby player from Premiership club Saracens.The Cricket Regulator launched an investigation into the circumstances surrounding the incident and said it examined a range of evidence before reaching its conclusion.“Having carefully reviewed all currently available information, the Cricket Regulator has determined that there is insufficient evidence to establish that any regulatory breach occurred,” the body said in a statement.According to the regulator, investigators pursued “multiple lines of inquiry to gather and assess evidence” before deciding that the case did not warrant further action.The episode reignited debate around the culture within the England men's setup, particularly after criticism that followed the team's unsuccessful Ashes tour of Australia, where reports of excessive drinking and unprofessional behaviour had attracted widespread attention.While the off-field issue has now been resolved, attention will quickly shift back to cricket. England and New Zealand head into the third and final Test with the series level at 1-1, setting up a decisive showdown at Trent Bridge.
Article Summary: England captain Ben Stokes and fast bowler Gus Atkinson have been cleared of any wrongdoing following an investigation into a nightclub incident. The pair had already been handed written warnings by the England and Wales Cricket Board for breaching a team curfew. Both players are expected to return for the series- deciding third Test, which begins on Thursday at Trent Bridge.

Article Title: India’s U-19 women team: New girls on the block have their day
Article Text:
India U-19 women's captain Bhavika Ahire (l) and all-rounder Nidhi Mahto

CHENNAI: The afternoon sun was unforgiving, but India’s next generation of cricketers looked at home in the heat at the MA Chidambaram Stadium. India’s Under-19 women’s team found two standout performers in debutant all-rounder Nidhi Mahto and newly-appointed captain Bhavika Ahire, whose composed partnership laid the foundation for a commanding victory over Sri Lanka in the first of the three-match T20 series.With India reeling at 26-2, the duo stitched together a match-defining 128-run partnership off 83 deliveries. Mahto struck a fluent 59 off 44 balls, peppered with eight boundaries and a six, while Ahire led from the front in her first game as captain with a 47-ball 73, featuring 11 fours and a maximum, guiding India to 170-6 in 20 overs.The dominance did not end there. Sri Lanka were bundled out for 77 in 18.3 overs, with Mahto starring with the ball too, returning exceptional figures of 3-5 in her 1.3 overs.Yet, beyond the numbers, the innings reflected clarity of thought and maturity. "Our thoughts were clear about batting. We were trying to build a partnership and focus on what the situation was demanding,” Ahire said after the match.Echoing her captain, Mahto added, “We wanted to take it till the end. As long as the partnership is there, the scoreboard will keep ticking.”For Mahto, the performance was another chapter in a rapid rise through the ranks.The Delhi youngster was the leading wicket-taker in the Women’s Delhi Premier League last year, claiming nine wickets in four matches for Central Delhi Queens.For her wearing the India jersey for the first time was a special feeling. “I felt good. I got a good performance in my debut match and I will continue to do well in the future as well,” she said confidently.Interestingly, Mahto was not always a leg-spinner. She began her cricketing journey as a medium pacer before switching disciplines just a few years ago. “I started bowling leg-spin two to three years ago. I also started batting one to two years ago,” she revealed.The transformation was largely driven by her coach. “My coach told me to try batting and it worked out well. For leg-spin, I was bowling in the nets and my coach praised me and told me to continue. I practised it and it became good.”The inspiration behind her bowling, meanwhile, comes from one of cricket 's greatest artists. "I used to watch Shane Warne's bowling. My father showed me his bowling. I liked his googlies and variations and from then I started trying it," she said.While Warne influences her bowling, India's batting star Smriti Mandhana remains her role model with the bat.Ahire, too, looks up to Indian stalwarts. Alongside Mandhana, she draws inspiration from Virat Kohli's ability to finish games. "Like he chases targets, I also want to chase such scores and stay till the end," Ahire said.As for captaincy, Ahire is still discovering her own style. "I don't have any captaincy inspiration. I am getting used to it," she said.The Maharashtra batter was part of the Under-19 Indian team which won the World Cup in 2025. This time, however, she carries greater responsibility as captain and one of the senior members of the side."It is an added responsibility, but when I am batting, I keep captaincy aside and play my game. Our team has good players and every player backing each other gives us confidence," she said.Preparations for the next Under-19 Women's World Cup have already begun in earnest, with Ahire highlighting the importance of the Centre of Excellence camps and team bonding sessions. "When we played the World Cup last year, I kept a few points with me. We will try to win every game," she said.Both Ahire and Mahto also credited the senior players. "The big players like Smriti and Gautami Naik, who has played in the WPL (Women's Premier League), have helped us learn a lot. The more we learn, the better it is for us," they said.
Article Summary: India's Under-19 women's team beat Sri Lanka in first of three-match T20 series. All-rounder Nidhi Mahto and newly-appointed captain Bhavika Ahire starred. Mahto struck 59 off 44 balls, peppered with eight boundaries and a six. Ahire led from the front in her first game as captain with a 47-ball 73.

Article Title: 'Don't try to change it': Vaibhav Sooryavanshi warned by 81-year-old cricket legend
Article Text:
Vaibhav Sooryavanshi (X-Cricbuzz)

Poll Which legendary bowler do you think Sooryavanshi would find most challenging? Michael Holding Joel Garner Andy Roberts Colin Croft

Praise for Vaibhav Sooryavanshi continues to pour in from some of cricket's biggest names, and the latest endorsement has come from legendary former West Indies captain Clive Lloyd , who believes the teenage sensation possesses the qualities needed for a bright future at the highest level.Lloyd, one of the most respected figures in cricket history and the man who captained the West Indies to World Cup triumphs in 1975 and 1979, was recently asked how the 15-year-old Indian batter would have coped against the fearsome pace attack that dominated world cricket during his era.The 81-year-old led a side featuring some of the game's most intimidating fast bowlers, including Michael Holding Joel Garner , Andy Roberts and Colin Croft. Reflecting on a hypothetical contest between Sooryavanshi and those legendary quicks, Lloyd felt it would have been a fascinating battle.“Well, he would have had a very good test (laughs), and we would be testing his character, his skills, and so on. He has good eyesight. He is a good striker of the ball. Yeah, so it would be a good test for him and a good test for our bowlers. The point about the bowlers that we had, why it was difficult to decipher what they were doing, because they all did something different. They didn't do the same thing. So, it would have been a great test,” Lloyd said in an interview with RevSportz.While praising the youngster's talent, Lloyd also offered an important piece of advice regarding his development. The former West Indies great believes Sooryavanshi's natural approach should be preserved rather than altered as he progresses through the ranks.“I think this guy's got ability, and I'm sure that if he continues to play in the manner that he is playing, and that they don't try to change it, you know, his style of play, I think we have a young man that we will be watching for quite a long while,” he added.Lloyd's comments come shortly after another extraordinary performance from the teenager. Representing India A in the tri-series final against Sri Lanka A in Dambulla on Sunday, Sooryavanshi produced a breathtaking innings that rewrote the record books.The left-hander smashed the fastest fifty in List A cricket history, reaching the milestone in just 11 deliveries. He looked on course for an equally remarkable century and could have matched the fastest hundred in List A cricket, but his innings ended on 94 from only 29 balls. A six off the final delivery he faced would have taken him to the landmark.Following his exploits in Sri Lanka, Sooryavanshi is set to travel to Ireland for India's upcoming two-match T20I series later this month. With an international debut widely expected during the tour, the teenager's rapid rise continues to gather momentum, and Lloyd's vote of confidence only adds to the growing excitement surrounding one of cricket's most talked-about young talents.
Article Summary: Former West Indies captain Clive Lloyd believes Vaibhav Sooryavanshi has the qualities needed for a bright future at the highest level. The 81-year-old led a side featuring some of the game's most intimidating fast bowlers, including Michael Holding Joel Garner, Andy Roberts and Colin Croft. Lloyd's comments come shortly after another extraordinary performance from the teenager.

Article Title: 'Sorry, Universe Boss': Kieron Pollard's message after breaking Chris Gayle's T20 record
Article Text:
Watch

Virat Kohli, Karan Aujla set the stage on fire in Delhi | Winning Speech bonus!
Article Summary: Virat Kohli, Karan Aujla set the stage on fire in Delhi. Winning Speech bonus! Virat's speech won him the Man of the Match award. Karan's speech also won him a man of the match award. Virat and Karan were the first Indian cricketers to win the award.

Article Title: Watch: Australia players recreate Dhoni-Yuvraj's iconic bike celebration after 3-0 Sweep over Bangladesh
Article Text:
Australia players recreate Dhoni-Yuvraj's iconic bike celebration

Poll Would you like to see more celebrations like Tim David bike ride in cricket? Yes, definitely No, they are unnecessary

NEW DELHI: Following Australia's dominant T20I series win over Bangladesh, Tim David stole the spotlight with an unusual celebration. After the match, the all-rounder rode a bright lime-green bike around the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium, with teammate Matt Renshaw sitting behind him.The pair took a victory lap as teammates cheered and fans recorded the moment, making it one of the highlights of the series.Nathan Ellis also joined the celebrations on a motorcycle, with Australia's bike parade evoking memories of MS Dhoni and Yuvraj Singh 's iconic victory lap during their playing days, a moment fondly remembered by cricket fans.Watch:Australia sealed a 3-0 sweep with a seven-wicket win in the third and final T20I. Chasing a modest target of 110, captain Mitchell Marsh smashed 60 off just 28 balls, helping the visitors wrap up the chase in only 11 overs.Josh Inglis chipped in with 17, while Tim David remained unbeaten on 12 and finished the game with two sixes.Australia had already won the first two matches and completed the whitewash with another commanding performance.“Nice to leave this series 3-0,” Marsh said. “I thought we executed well. We were fantastic with the ball today. Had some guys debuting, some coming back after a long time and contributing really well — great team to be around.”Australia's bowlers also impressed, with Spencer Johnson, Nathan Ellis and Adam Zampa picking up two wickets each.Bangladesh captain Towhid Hridoy was the standout performer for the hosts, scoring an unbeaten 61 as his side managed only 109 for eight after opting to bat first. Rishad Hossain was the only other batter to reach double figures with 16.“Wicket was good but we did not play well,” Hridoy said. “We needed partnerships, if we got partnerships, we could have got to 170-180.”Despite the T20I disappointment, Bangladesh had earlier claimed the three-match ODI series 2-1.
Article Summary: Australia beat Bangladesh by seven wickets in the third and final T20I. All- rounder Tim David rode a bike around the Sher-e-Bangla National Cricket Stadium. Australia had already won the first two matches and completed the whitewash with another commanding performance.

Article Title: 'Best thing is transfer': After win over Austria in FIFA World Cup, Argentina star says he wants to leave his club
Article Text:
Julian Alvarez (Photo, AP)

Argentina forward Julian Alvarez said he wants to leave La Liga club Atletico Madrid, putting his long-term future at the club under the spotlight despite their refusal to entertain transfer offers.Alvarez expressed his intentions after Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria in their second World Cup group match on Monday. The 26-year-old, who is also one of Argentina's key attacking players, admitted that he had already discussed the matter with the club."The best thing for everyone is a transfer," the 26-year-old told ESPN. "I don't think it's the right time to talk about this, but I can't hide either. I have to be honest, and I spoke with people at the club, the ones I needed to talk to."Soon after the interview was released in Spain, images of Atletico Madrid fans burning Alvarez's jersey surfaced on social media, reflecting the anger among sections of the club's supporters over his comments.Earlier this month, Real Madrid said Atletico Madrid had turned down an offer of 150 million euros (USD 171 million) for the Argentine forward. The bid was part of the promises made by Real Madrid president Florentino Perez during his re-election campaign.Spanish media reports have also linked FC Barcelona with a move for Álvarez, suggesting that several of Spain's biggest clubs remain interested in securing the services of the World Cup winner.Atletico Madrid has maintained that it is not willing to negotiate his transfer. Reports in Spain suggest Alvarez has a release clause worth 500 million euros (USD 571 million) in his contract, making any move extremely difficult unless the club changes its stance.Alvarez made his Atletico debut in August 2024 against Villarreal at the Estadio de la Ceramica and quickly established himself as an important member of Diego Simeone's squad.
Article Summary: Argentina forward Julian Alvarez says he wants to leave La Liga club Atletico Madrid. Alvarez expressed his intentions after Argentina's 2-0 win over Austria in their second World Cup group match on Monday. The 26-year-old admitted he had already discussed the matter with the club. Atletico has maintained that it is not willing to negotiate his transfer.

Article Title: Declan Rice reveals secret 'behind-the-scenes' struggle he had been dealing with that led to early World Cup substitution
Article Text:
England's Declan Rice talks to the media during a press conference ahead of the World Cup Group L soccer match between England and Ghana in Foxborough, Mass., near Boston, Monday, June 22, 2026. (AP Photo/Martin Meissner)

The injury problem England fans did not know about

A season that rarely stopped

Rice eases fears ahead of Ghana

Managing Bukayo Saka's recovery

England balancing ambition and fitness

England midfielder says he has been managing hamstring nerve pain since Christmas as he opens up on the physical toll of a 63-match season and reassures fans over his fitness for Ghana.England's opening victory over Croatia delivered almost everything Thomas Tuchel could have wanted from the start of a World Cup campaign. Harry Kane scored twice, Jude Bellingham and Marcus Rashford added further goals, and a 4-2 victory in Dallas immediately strengthened England's hopes of finishing top of Group L.The only concern arrived with 18 minutes remaining.Declan Rice, one of England's most important players and the midfielder around whom much of the team's balance is built, was withdrawn in the 72nd minute. Given the importance of the Arsenal midfielder and the timing of the substitution, concern quickly spread about whether England had suffered a significant injury setback after only one match of the tournament.Rice has now revealed that the decision was linked to an issue he has been quietly managing for more than six months.Far from being a new injury, the problem dates back to Christmas and has remained largely hidden from public view despite Rice continuing to play through one of the busiest seasons of his career.Speaking after the match, Rice explained that he has been dealing with neural pain in his hamstring since the middle of Arsenal's domestic campaign."I think it was a smart decision," Rice told ITV Sport."I was feeling a little bit of neural pain in my hamstring, which I was managing from after Christmas with Arsenal for a very long time."Obviously, not a lot of people would have known that. It was all behind the scenes stuff."The issue affects the upper hamstring area, with the pain radiating into his lower back, but Rice made clear that England's medical staff and coaching team viewed the substitution as a precaution rather than a response to a serious setback.The midfielder felt the timing was particularly important because the closing stages of matches are often when accumulated fatigue places the greatest strain on the body."But it was a smart decision," he said."In the end, that last 20 minutes is probably where you pick up the most, and it's where you play a 70-minute match, but that last 20 is where you really feel your body going through it."Rice's comments also provide an insight into the extraordinary demands placed on elite players during the modern football calendar.England's World Cup opener against Croatia was his 63rd match of the 2025-26 season.Fifty-five of those appearances came for Arsenal, who enjoyed one of the most successful campaigns in their recent history, winning the Premier League and reaching the Champions League final. Rice then added a further eight appearances for England before and during the World Cup.The cumulative workload has left little room for recovery.Reflecting on the schedule, Rice described the demands as relentless."It's an obscene amount of games," he said."The schedule was crazy but what can we do about it? You can't sit and complain."We have to just get on with it for the moments like I had in the Premier League, winning that Premier League."You know you'd play as many games as possible to have that feeling again and knowing that there's a World Cup at the end of it as well."You know you'd put your body on the line to be always in to play. It's a lot of games but we'll get our break at the end."Those comments underline why England were unwilling to take unnecessary risks against Croatia, particularly with two more group-stage fixtures still to play.The substitution immediately created questions about Rice's availability for England's second Group L match against Ghana.However, the midfielder moved quickly to reassure supporters that he remains fully available and expects to be involved."I'm ready, I'm fit, raring to go," he said."I think it was a smart decision because the last few days I felt really, really good."England know a victory over Ghana would put them in a commanding position to secure qualification for the knockout stages, making Rice's availability particularly significant.The midfielder has become one of the most influential players in the squad, providing defensive security while also helping England progress the ball through midfield.Rice also addressed the fitness situation surrounding his Arsenal and England teammate Bukayo Saka.Saka missed the start against Croatia as he continues to manage a longstanding Achilles issue that has required careful monitoring throughout recent months.The winger did not train with the main group on Saturday, instead following an individual programme designed to manage his workload and ensure he remains available later in the tournament.Although he is not expected to start against Ghana, Rice believes England are handling the situation correctly and remains confident that Saka will play an important role during the World Cup."Hundred percent," Rice said when asked whether Saka would still have a major part to play."It's really good the way we're managing him."The amount of football he's played, I've seen him at Arsenal with this little Achilles problem he's got. We're managing him the right way for sure."Saka showed his quality against Croatia despite only appearing as a substitute, creating Marcus Rashford's goal after entering the match in the second half.England's preparation for the tournament included a heat-acclimatisation camp in Florida, with Tuchel and his staff placing a strong emphasis on ensuring the squad could cope with the conditions they may face across North America.While the Croatia match was played inside Dallas' air-conditioned stadium with temperatures set around 22C, England expect more demanding conditions later in the tournament.Rice said the players have been reassured by Tuchel that the squad possesses the physical conditioning required to maintain their aggressive style regardless of the weather."The heat's going to dictate [our playing style] in spells," Rice explained."But the manager's been really clear with us and with this squad that we have the strength and power to outrun and outplay teams with our strength."For now, England's immediate focus remains Ghana and securing early progression from Group L. Rice's revelation offers a clearer explanation for the concern generated by his substitution against Croatia, while also highlighting the physical reality behind a season that has already stretched to 63 matches.The encouraging news for England is that the midfielder who has quietly managed that burden since Christmas insists he is fit, available and ready to continue.
Article Summary: England's Declan Rice says he has been managing hamstring nerve pain since Christmas. Midfielder has played 63 times for Arsenal and eight times for England this season. Rice has become one of England's most influential players at the World Cup. Arsenal winger Bukayo Saka continues to recover from Achilles injury.

Article Title: FIFA World Cup: Egypt’s momentum hit by roadblock as Seattle travel plan rejected
Article Text:
Egypt's players during a training session. (AP Photo)

Travel plan rejected by authorities



Historic win overshadowed by logistical challenge



Egypt’s preparations for their decisive FIFA World Cup Group G clash against Iran have been disrupted after local security authorities rejected a request to allow the squad to travel directly from Vancouver to Seattle, according to a Reuters report.The decision comes just days after Egypt celebrated a landmark moment in their football history, securing their first-ever World Cup victory with a commanding 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand at BC Place in Vancouver on Sunday.The team had planned to move key players directly to Seattle following the match as part of an effort to streamline travel ahead of Friday’s crucial fixture. However, that request was denied, forcing the squad to revise its logistical arrangements.“The security authorities refused the team's request to stay in the city of Seattle as planned after the New Zealand match in the World Cup, and therefore the team's delegation will return to the city of Spokane,” Hossam said in a statement issued by the Egyptian FA, as quoted by Reuters.Instead of going to Seattle, Egypt will now return to its training base in Spokane — approximately 280 miles away — before making the journey back for the Iran match.The adjustment adds an extra layer of travel fatigue ahead of a high-pressure encounter that could decide qualification for the Round of 32.Egypt’s travel issue comes on the heels of a dramatic performance against New Zealand, where they overturned an early deficit to claim a landmark victory.New Zealand started brightly and took the lead through Finn Surman, who headed home from a Tim Payne corner after early pressure. Egypt, however, gradually grew into the contest, with Mohamed Marmoush and Mohamed Salah both going close before halftime.The breakthrough arrived just before the hour mark when Mostafa Ziko levelled with a close-range header from Mohamed Hany’s cross. Buoyed by the equaliser, Egypt took control as Salah finished clinically after a sharp exchange inside the box, before Trezeguet sealed the win with a header from a corner in the 82nd minute.The result moved Egypt close to qualification from Group G, but their build-up to the Iran clash is now complicated by unexpected travel disruption rather than momentum alone.
Article Summary: Travel plan rejected by authorities for Egypt's World Cup clash with Iran. Egypt had planned to move key players directly to Seattle following the match as part of an effort to streamline travel ahead of Friday’s crucial fixture. The decision comes just days after Egypt secured their first-ever World Cup victory with a commanding 3-1 comeback win over New Zealand.

Article Title: Who will Kylian Mbappé and France face in the 2026 FIFA World Cup Round of 32? Possible opponents explored
Article Text:
Who Will France Play in the Round of 32? (Reuters)

Who could France face if they finish top of Group I?



What happens if France finish second in Group I?



Is winning Group I important for France's World Cup hopes?



How is Kylian Mbappé influencing France's knockout ambitions?



France have secured their place in the World Cup Round of 32 after beating Iraq in a dramatic contest that was interrupted by a 131-minute weather delay in Philadelphia. The result left Didier Deschamps' side in control of Group I, but not yet certain of finishing first. With Norway still mathematically capable of overtaking them, France's final group position remains unresolved. That uncertainty carries significant consequences, not only for their immediate opponent but also for the route they could face deeper into the tournament as Kylian Mbappé continues his prolific scoring run.The most attractive outcome for France appears to be winning the group. A first-place finish would send Les Bleus to New York on June 30, where they would meet one of the tournament's strongest third-placed teams.Several possible opponents are already emerging from that pool. Scotland, Paraguay and Sweden are among the leading candidates, while Belgium or Iran could come from Group G. Group H may also provide either Cape Verde or Uruguay depending on the final standings.At the moment, projections suggest Sweden are the most likely opponent, with Japan and Ivory Coast also featuring among the leading possibilities. The final picture, however, will not become clear until all group-stage matches are completed.For France, topping the group would not simply be about avoiding a stronger Round of 32 opponent. It would also place them on a far more favorable side of the knockout bracket.A runner-up finish would send France to Dallas instead of New York and create a completely different challenge.Rather than facing a third-placed team, they would meet the second-placed nation from Group E. The contenders currently include Ivory Coast, Ecuador and Curazao.None of those teams can be taken lightly, particularly in a knockout setting where margins are often small. More importantly, finishing second would dramatically alter France's long-term prospects in the competition.The route becomes significantly tougher almost immediately, increasing the likelihood of facing elite opposition much earlier than expected.The answer appears to be yes.If France finish first and successfully navigate the Round of 32, they would likely move into a Round of 16 matchup against either the winner of Group G or the runner-up from Group H. On paper, that side of the bracket offers more manageable challenges and a clearer route toward the latter stages.A group victory would also keep France on track for a potential last-16 meeting with Germany, who have already secured top spot in Group E.That scenario is hardly easy, but it avoids several of the tournament's biggest contenders until later rounds.France's position has been strengthened by the form of their captain.Mbappé scored twice against Iraq despite the extraordinary delay caused by thunderstorms at Lincoln Financial Field. The goals took his tournament tally to four and lifted his overall World Cup total to 16.That achievement places him just two goals behind Lionel Messi on the competition's all-time scoring list after the Argentina captain also struck twice earlier in the day.More importantly for France, Mbappé is finding form at exactly the right time. As the knockout phase approaches, his ability to decide matches in moments could prove decisive regardless of where France ultimately finish in Group I.
Article Summary: France have secured their place in the World Cup Round of 32 after beating Iraq. The result left Didier Deschamps' side in control of Group I, but not yet certain of finishing first. A first-place finish would send France to New York on June 30, where they would meet one of the tournament's strongest third-placed teams.

Article Title: NFL host Kay Adams fires back after someone secretly snapped a viral photo of her at a NASCAR race
Article Text:
NFL host Kay Adams fires back after someone secretly snapped a viral photo of her at a NASCAR race(Getty)

Kay Adams joins the joke after NASCAR snapshot sparks fan frenzy

Kay Adams stepped away from the NFL world and surprisingly found herself in a very different spotlight.
